data: NO
We both finish eating our noodles and say goodbye to each other.
The walk back home is lonely and the stars glisten once more.
Falling tears glisten alongside them, making the ground their last stop.
I look at my feet while I stand in front of my room.
Drops falling on them while a single question invades my mind:
“Am I really good enough for [Paul]?”
Another day comes, and with it, I have to pretend to be okay.
I arrive to my class, and notice [Paul] playing a video game once more.
[He] looks at me and greets me as if nothing had happened.
I play along and sit beside [him]. Still, I feel like I want to leave things clear.
[Paul], listen, I need to tell you that-
Unexpectedly, a group of students come barging in
YO, YO, YO!!! HOW’S EVERYBODY DOING?
The volleyball team comes here to remind you about the annual VOLLEY TOURNAMENT!!!!
ALL of you must make a team and participate if you don’t want to fail your classes
so… Register online right now!
And remember the winning team gets extra points! Buh-bye!
With that, they go away while cheering.
I mean, it's great that Aukademi promotes physical health to their students,
but do they really have to make it obligatory to pass the semester?
That’s just straight up mean.
Dang, guess we’ll have to find a team, right?
Got anybody in mind?
Um… maybe…
Suddenly, a friendly girl approaches us.
‘Sup buddies! My friends and I are planning to make a team
and we’re missing just two players! Wanna join?
Sure! Why not?
Great! We’ll make a plan soon, see ya later.
The girl just walked away, back to her friends.
Well, at least we’ve got a team now.
Last year I had to play with the ones nobody wanted…
Needless to say, we lost.
I look out the open window, and enjoy the slight breeze that hits my face.
I close my eyes and feel it against my skin.
Um, [tn]?
I look at [Paul], a little bit surprised.
He then shows me his game
two little avatars that look exactly like us are standing next to each other in front of a big house.
I made us
I make you visit me sometimes, and right now I’m making us fall in love…
Do you like it?
My heart skips a beat when I see this.
Did he really take the time to do this for me?
Something about looking at us in there makes me feel all giddy.
How long did this take you?
Like 10 hours? I’m not that sure
but It’s just that it was kinda hard to find some cosmetics that made the little version of you look like,
well, you.
Aww, thanks [Paul], this is so sweet.
Fabian wrapped [his] arm around me and pulled me closer.
You’re the sweet one.
[Paul] then grabs my face and makes me look at [him].
My eyes are wide open as [he] leans in and kisses me on the lips.
I look around, worried that someone might see us, but everybody is way too busy on their own business anyways.
I let myself close my eyes and melt into the kiss.
He used to hate it when I got romantic in front of so many people.
But this kiss only shows me that [Paul] can change for me…
And I as well can.
A month had already passed, and everything was going so well!
[Paul] had done a complete 180, and was acting as loving and caring as ever.
One day he even brought me flowers!
We also have spent a lot of time training for the tournament with our team.
[Paul] wasn’t very excited about it at first,
but as we practiced and got better, [he] got filled with a sporty spirit.
As of now, it is the morning of the tournament.
I am just finishing getting ready when I hear my familiar hardwired friend call out to me.
Oh, [tn]! Breakfast is ready!
You need your protein if you want to win!
Coming, Helpie! Just give me a minute.
I quickly tie my shoes and run to the kitchen, getting fully ready for the tournament.
I look at the delicious breakfast Helpie had made me and I give them a nervous smile.
Something wrong, [tn]? I sense your heartbeat quickening.
Well, y’know… It’s just the nerves of the competition…
I really don’t want to disappoint my team
Feeling like that is completely normal [tn].
It’s a big event, but it’s really no big deal.
Just by competing you are already doing your part, the rest is just pure fun!
Don’t be hard on yourself, okay? Especially with your injury from last training.
I gulp my last bite of breakfast and look at my leg.
It still hurt from the fall I had last week, but I felt quite better as of now!
In fact, I feel like nothing could stop me!
Right. It’s okay Helpie, I promise I’ll be good and won’t hurt myself.
Now, thanks for the encouragement! But I’ve gotta go! See you!
See you later [tn]. If you need anything, don’t forget to ask!
Yup. Buh-bye!
I quickly walk towards the school gym.
I try to keep my breath steady to drown my nerves, although Helpie has really helped me calm down most of them.
I arrive at the gym and most of my team is already there.
[tn]! Over here! Let’s warm up while the tournament starts.
As I go up to them, I look at [Paul] and greet [him].
[He] looks at me and smiles, but there is something that is definitely bugging [him].
I can see it in [his] face.
You’re late [tn].
Uh, hi… But I arrived 30 minutes before the tournament starts.
And? You always need to come early.
To plan and make sure you’re not gonna mess up.
Before I could protest, the friendly girl came up to us and claps her hands.
Gather around love birds. We’re going to pass the ball.
[Paul]'s demeanor suddenly changes completely.
[He] grabs me by my hand and leads me to all our other teammates.
We pass the ball, we stretch and overall just have fun while waiting.
After a while, the tournament finally begins.
We play a couple matches and make it through! Although it was actually kind of
easy since a lot of teams weren’t really that motivated.
They for sure just participated to avoid failing.
After some more matches, and making our way to the finals, my team and I celebrated,
but also felt a little bit of pressure. We were so close to getting extra points in class,
how could you let such an opportunity pass?
The team gathers around for one last time and we ready ourselves for the last match
Okay guys, this is it! No pressure
but if we get some extra credit, that would be sweet, haha!
Still, let’s have fun, yeah? 1,2,3, let 's go!
With that, we all get into our position and prepare ourselves.
I can feel the fire in my eyes as my muscles tense up
I look at [Paul], and he nods at me, telling me he trusts in me.
The match starts, and the ball starts going from one place to another.
My teammates swiftly hit the ball and pass it to the other side, until the ball heads straight to me.
I prepare myself and jump.
I hit the ball and manage to score a point. But as my legs touch the ground again,
my injured leg sends a sharp pain to my brain.
I hiss at the pain, but I can’t let this be the reason we lose. I won’t be that reason.
Time flies by, and both teams have already won two sets.
This set was the one that defined everything.
All the other students cheered for their favorite team,
and I’d be wrong to say that we weren’t vastly cheered for.
With every hit, we were closer to win,
but I felt my leg’s condition worsen every time I stepped on the ground with a little too much force.
I can see Helpie in the back of my mind, telling me not to overwork myself.
But when I turn to the right,
I see [Paul], telling me to not make a single mistake with [his] eyes.
The game was so intense that the decision depended on a match point.
Adrenaline pumps through my veins, and the final match begins.
The ball swiftly flies from one side to another without touching the ground or going out of the court.
Everybody’s soul is as fiery as a bonfire.
One of the players on the other team hits the ball with a huge amount of force, I know I can reach it if I
run with all my might, else, we were definitely going to lose.
My legs move on their own and the ball flies straight to me.
I am about to hit the ball when I hear [Paul]’s voice.
Hit it, [tn]! HIT IT!
Hearing [Paul] makes me lose my focus, but worse,
I take a wrong step and feel something in my leg break.
AAAGH!!!
Everybody shouts my name as I fall to the ground and grab my leg in pain.
I wince while a medic comes to check me.
I look at my teammates and everybody is hovering over me with worry in their faces. Everyone but [Paul].
I feel [his] cold gaze lay upon me, making my body sweat.
The paramedic finishes putting a bandage on my leg.
He says I pulled my muscle, but with good care, I would be all right.
Sadly, we lost the match. But it was a nice experience overall.
[tn], I can’t believe this happened to you! I’m so sorry,
I should’ve told you to rest the moment you fell while training!
It’s okay, really. I wanted to play anyway, so… It's my fault too!
Still, you played amazingly! Great work everyone!
I hope we can play again together someday!
And [Paul], take good care of [tn], will you?
[Paul] helped me get up, and got under my arm as support.
No need to say it twice.
We all say our goodbyes and head our separate ways.
[Paul]helps me walk all the way to the back of the gym, even if [he] has to carry most of my weight.
I’m sorry [Paul]. But thank you for helping me out.
I really wish I didn’t injure my leg, else, we would’ve- AAH.
Suddenly, I feel my butt make contact with the harsh floor.
I look up, and [Paul] is looking at me with disdain.
It’s your fault.
What?
I said, It’s your fault that we lost.
If only you had made an actual effort to win…
[Paul], I’m injured.
Look at my leg, Is this not enough for you?
NO! Ugh… You don’t understand…
If we fail this class, it’s gonna be YOUR fault.
We could’ve been all set with extra points, but YOU-
[Paul] turned around and grabbed [his] head in desperation.
I just held my leg once again, It started hurting once again with the fall.
I hope you figure out how to get back to your house,
because I’m leaving you here just as you left our team.
[Paul]! [Paul], wait!
[He] left me there. Injured and alone.
I feel my eyes overflow, and my lip quiver as I see the night is arriving.
I gather all my strength and use the walls to help myself up.
Then, I begin my walk home.
I use all the strength that I have left, and manage to arrive.
I knock on the door and wait for Helpie to open it.
Hello [tn]. How was the tourna-
Helpie sees my bandage and it seemed like all their circuits went crazy.
They carried me all the way to my bed and sat me gently on the edge of it.
[tn]! You’re hurt! I’ll scan you to make sure everything’s okay.
I chuckle and shake my head.
I’m fine Helpie. The paramedic said it’s just a pulled muscle
so everything’s alright. I’ll be good in a week, maybe.
Helpie ended up scanning my leg and quickly brought me some new bandages and a pomade.
They then started putting it in my leg and wrapping it up.
[tn], and how come none of your teammates helped you come here?
Not even [Paul]?
NO
